bins.name: Enforceable transition protections
bins.desc: DCC calls for explicit grandfathering and legally enforceable transition provisions in the final rule so that projects with existing commitments under current rules are not disrupted by prospective reforms.
quotes: DCC's Initial Comments strongly advocated for "clear and legally enforceable transition provisions" to ensure that reforms aimed at improving future processes do not disrupt projects that have made significant commitments under existing rules.
The Commission should incorporate explicit grandfathering and transition pathways in any final rule to provide this essential certainty.

bins.name: Hybrid facility rights clarity
bins.desc: DCC urges FERC to resolve the inconsistent treatment of hybrid load-plus-generation facilities by using the existing PJM co-location record and extending resulting principles through this rulemaking. DCC also opposes making generation pairing mandatory.
quotes: DCC urged the Commission to act on the "complete record on co-location issues in PJM" to "deliver immediate clarity."

Resolving this issue in the existing dockets, and extending principles nationally through this rulemaking, is the most direct way to unlock the significant system benefits that hybrid configurations offer and to provide the certainty developers require.
pairing of load with generation must remain a voluntary incentive, not a requirement, to avoid unjust and unreasonable discrimination.

bins.name: Integrated load-generation studies
bins.desc: DCC supports studying new load and new generation together to capture system benefits, reduce duplicative studies, and align net impact with interconnection costs.
quotes: DCC advocated for studying new load and new generation together to capture system benefits.

bins.name: Expedited study scope
bins.desc: DCC supports expedited interconnection study for configurations that deliver demonstrable grid benefits, and argues that expedited pathways should not be limited to curtailable loads since operational requirements may preclude curtailability.
quotes: Support exists for creating expedited pathways for configurations that provide demonstrable grid benefits, with many commenters emphasizing the need for such pathways to accommodate the diverse operational profiles of large loads.

expedited processes should not be limited to curtailable loads, as this may be incompatible with operational requirements, and that coordination of new load and incremental generation alone warrants expedited study.

bins.name: Study process reform
bins.desc: DCC supports integrated interconnection studies and broader transmission planning reform, framing interconnection delays as a symptom of insufficient infrastructure rather than a standalone procedural problem.
quotes: interconnection reform is a prerequisite for U.S. leadership in AI, advanced manufacturing, and the digital economy
the goal of this proceeding must be to "accelerate—not pause—critical load and generation projects already advancing under existing frameworks."
DCC advocated for studying new load and new generation together to capture system benefits.
interconnection delays as a "symptom of a deeper and more structural issue"—a lack of sufficient transmission infrastructure and forward-looking planning.

DCC's call for the Commission to "focus on the infrastructure challenge through appropriate long-term transmission planning" and to require deployment of grid-enhancing technologies is a necessary component of any effective interconnection reform effort.

bins.name: Co-location and market access balance
bins.desc: DCC supports streamlined pathways for hybrid co-located facilities while insisting that stand-alone load must retain access to competitive wholesale markets and that generation pairing remain voluntary, not mandatory.
quotes: DCC urged the Commission to act on the "complete record on co-location issues in PJM" to "deliver immediate clarity."
DCC advocated for studying new load and new generation together to capture system benefits.

Resolving this issue in the existing dockets, and extending principles nationally through this rulemaking, is the most direct way to unlock the significant system benefits that hybrid configurations offer and to provide the certainty developers require.
a durable framework "must allow load to interconnect efficiently, whether or not that load is paired with on-site supply."
pairing of load with generation must remain a voluntary incentive, not a requirement, to avoid unjust and unreasonable discrimination.
Any new interconnection framework must preserve the ability of load to rely on these "competitive markets whose design the Commission has spent more than two decades refining."

bins.name: Flexible load expedited pathways
bins.desc: DCC supports expedited study for flexible or paired configurations but argues the pathway should not exclude non-curtailable loads, since coordination of new load and incremental generation alone justifies expedited treatment.
quotes: Support exists for creating expedited pathways for configurations that provide demonstrable grid benefits, with many commenters emphasizing the need for such pathways to accommodate the diverse operational profiles of large loads.

expedited processes should not be limited to curtailable loads, as this may be incompatible with operational requirements, and that coordination of new load and incremental generation alone warrants expedited study.

bins.name: PJM co-location record
bins.desc: DCC specifically cites the pending PJM co-location proceeding (Docket Nos. EL25-49, EL25-20, AD24-11) as a ready record FERC should resolve quickly, then extend the resulting principles nationally.
quotes: DCC urged the Commission to act on the "complete record on co-location issues in PJM" to "deliver immediate clarity."

Resolving this issue in the existing dockets, and extending principles nationally through this rulemaking, is the most direct way to unlock the significant system benefits that hybrid configurations offer and to provide the certainty developers require.

bins.name: National strategic need
bins.desc: DCC argues that interconnection reform is directly tied to U.S. leadership in AI, advanced manufacturing, and the digital economy, framing timely action as a national security and economic competitiveness priority.
quotes: interconnection reform is a prerequisite for U.S. leadership in AI, advanced manufacturing, and the digital economy

bins.name: Grandfathering in-flight projects
bins.desc: DCC calls for binding grandfathering and transition pathways so that new rules apply only prospectively and do not disrupt projects already under study, construction, or with executed interconnection agreements.
quotes: the goal of this proceeding must be to "accelerate—not pause—critical load and generation projects already advancing under existing frameworks."

DCC's Initial Comments strongly advocated for "clear and legally enforceable transition provisions" to ensure that reforms aimed at improving future processes do not disrupt projects that have made significant commitments under existing rules.
The Commission should incorporate explicit grandfathering and transition pathways in any final rule to provide this essential certainty.

bins.name: Stand-alone load market rights
bins.desc: DCC insists that any new framework must preserve stand-alone load access to competitive wholesale markets and that co-location with on-site generation must remain a voluntary choice, not a condition of interconnection.
quotes: a durable framework "must allow load to interconnect efficiently, whether or not that load is paired with on-site supply."
pairing of load with generation must remain a voluntary incentive, not a requirement, to avoid unjust and unreasonable discrimination.
Any new interconnection framework must preserve the ability of load to rely on these "competitive markets whose design the Commission has spent more than two decades refining."

bins.name: Transmission infrastructure reform
bins.desc: DCC frames interconnection bottlenecks as a symptom of inadequate transmission infrastructure and calls on FERC to pursue long-term transmission planning and require deployment of grid-enhancing technologies in parallel with interconnection reform.
quotes: interconnection delays as a "symptom of a deeper and more structural issue"—a lack of sufficient transmission infrastructure and forward-looking planning.

DCC's call for the Commission to "focus on the infrastructure challenge through appropriate long-term transmission planning" and to require deployment of grid-enhancing technologies is a necessary component of any effective interconnection reform effort.
